creator: beyert@cs.ucr.edu
text:
I am the current maintainer of math/isabelle.

It gives me great pains to say this, but math/isabelle is too hard to maintain and keep up to date, so I think it would be best if someone else takes over the port, or that it is dropped from the ports tree entirely, since the isabelle developers don't like third-party ports or packages (the old versions like this one are not especially hard to build from source, at least).

At some point I would like to bring this port back from the dead with a current version, but I don't have a timeframe on when that would happen.  For now, I'd like to focus more heavily on ML language ports that I use more frequently, such as lang/mlton, lang/urweb, lang/twelf and lang/polyml, especially the first two.

I would advise anyone up for the task of maintaining math/isabelle to look into bringing back polyml support, since it compiles more quickly, making it less likely to time out on port build servers.
